title: carduus arvensis
intro: Carduus arvensis, also known as the field thistle or wild artichoke, is a biennial or perennial plant species that belongs to the sunflower family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-diarrheal effects. The health benefits of Carduus arvensis include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of menopausal hot flashes and menstrual cramps. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in herbal capsules and supplements.
